concerns.text: Task T015 is marked [X] (complete) but contains the comment '<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->'. This indicates the task deliverable (integration of novel object generation with evaluation) is non-functional or undefined, violating FR-003 (generate diverse novel objects) and FR-005 (paired t-test) which require valid input data. A task marked complete must satisfy its acceptance criteria.

Task T007a (Execute generator to produce novel geometry files) is marked [ ] (incomplete). FR-003 and SC-001/SC-002 require a 'NovelObjectSet' of 30 generated geometries for zero-shot evaluation. Without this execution task being completed, the core evaluation dataset is undefined, rendering the statistical comparison (FR-005) impossible.

Task T029 (Run full end-to-end benchmark, log metrics, assert time/memory limits) is marked [ ] (incomplete). SC-003 (6h limit) and SC-004 (7GB RAM) are measurable outcomes that require this specific verification step. Leaving this task incomplete means the success criteria for CPU-tractability are not verified, violating the spec's measurable outcomes.

Task T009a (Implement checksum verification logic) is marked [ ] (incomplete). Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) mandates that datasets be checksummed and recorded. The absence of this implementation task means the project cannot enforce data integrity or versioning discipline as required by the constitution.

concerns.location: tasks.md:Phase 1, T001, T002, T003, T009, T024
concerns.text: Multiple foundational tasks (T001-T003, T009, T024) are marked [ ] (incomplete). Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) requires a runnable project structure, pinned dependencies, and CI enforcement. Without these, the project cannot be executed or verified, violating the non-negotiable reproducibility constraint.
